title: gnetidae
intro: The rank of Gnetidae refers to a group of flowering plant orders that are not closely related to other flowering plant orders. It is a clade of plants that are often considered to be of uncertain affinities within the plant kingdom. Some examples of orders within the Gnetidae include Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales, which comprise a diverse range of plant families, including grasses, flowering plants, and more. The Gnetidae is a distinct taxonomic group that highlights the complexity and diversity of plant evolution.

orders.order_desc: Gnetales belong to the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Gnetales, family Gnetales, order Gnetales, genus Gnetales, and species Gnetales.
